commodities.name: Palm Oil
commodities.deforestation_link: Leading driver of tropical deforestation — plantations replace rainforest, destroying habitat for orangutans, tigers, and thousands of species.
commodities.scale: Responsible for ~8% of global deforestation (2015-2018). Over 10 million hectares of tropical forest cleared for palm since 1990.

commodities.name: Cocoa
commodities.deforestation_link: Major driver of deforestation in West Africa — Ghana and Côte d'Ivoire have lost millions of hectares of forest to cocoa expansion.
commodities.scale: Cocoa farming has caused ~2-3 million hectares of deforestation in West Africa. Côte d'Ivoire has lost 80% of its forests since 1960.

commodities.name: Soy
commodities.deforestation_link: Soy expansion drives deforestation in South America, particularly Brazil's Cerrado and Amazon regions. Mostly used for animal feed, not directly in food.
commodities.scale: ~480,000 hectares deforested annually for soy in tropical countries. Second largest driver of deforestation globally.

commodities.name: Coffee
commodities.deforestation_link: Sun-grown coffee replaces forest canopy. Shade-grown coffee is less destructive but most commercial production uses sun-grown methods.
commodities.scale: 2.5 million hectares of forest cleared for coffee since 2000, primarily in tropical highlands.

commodities.name: Tea
commodities.deforestation_link: Tea plantations can expand into forested areas, though the link is less direct than palm oil or cocoa.
commodities.scale: Lower deforestation impact than other commodities but still contributes to habitat conversion in some regions.

commodities.name: Dairy / Cattle Feed (indirect)
commodities.deforestation_link: Dairy cows are often fed imported soy. The product itself doesn't contain deforestation-risk ingredients, but the supply chain does.
commodities.scale: Indirect link — European livestock consumes ~30 million tonnes of soy annually, much of it from deforestation-risk regions.

companies.name: Ferrero Group
companies.traceability_summary: 100% RSPO Segregated palm oil, traceable to mill level. Working toward plantation-level traceability.
companies.ngos_score: WWF Palm Oil Buyers Scorecard 2020: ranked #1 out of 173 companies

companies.name: Mondelēz International
companies.traceability_summary: Farm-level GPS mapping in Ghana and Côte d'Ivoire. Claims near-zero deforestation on Cocoa Life farms. However, mass balance purchasing means certified and uncertified cocoa can mix.

incidents.summary: Investigation found child labour and illegal deforestation in Ghana farms supplying Mondelēz
Investigation linked Mondelēz to new deforestation in Liberia through opaque cocoa supply chains. Mondelēz did not respond to requests for comment.

companies.ngos_score: Ethical Consumer: zero points for climate action. Chocolate Scorecard: middling.

companies.name: Tony's Chocolonely
companies.traceability_summary: 100% traceable to cooperative level, largely to individual farmers. 26,536 farms GPS-polygon mapped. Bean Tracker platform provides container-level traceability.
companies.ngos_score: Chocolate Scorecard: ranked #1 for 5 consecutive years (91% in 2025)

companies.name: Kellanova (formerly Kellogg's)
companies.traceability_summary: Traceable to refinery level. Mix of RSPO Segregated and Mass Balance. Previously relied on GreenPalm certificates (credits). Independent auditor Proforest engaged.

incidents.summary: Pringles in China confirmed to use palm oil from Wilmar plantations linked to labour abuses

companies.ngos_score: Palm Oil Detectives: recommends boycott. Kellogg's scored better than many peers on NDPE commitment.

companies.name: pladis (Yıldız Holding)
companies.traceability_summary: Working toward mill-level traceability since 2018. Uses Starling satellite monitoring. Limited public disclosure.
companies.ngos_score: Ethical Consumer: worst rating in biscuits category

companies.name: Unilever
companies.traceability_summary: 97% of key commodities independently verified deforestation-free (2024). Founding RSPO member. Satellite monitoring across 20M+ hectares. Missed original 2020 deadline.

incidents.summary: Criticised Unilever's palm oil suppliers for continued deforestation links

companies.ngos_score: Industry leader in policy, but missed deforestation-free deadlines (2020 → 2023 → 97% by 2024)

companies.name: The Coca-Cola Company
companies.deforestation_policy: World Without Waste strategy (packaging-focused, not ingredients)
companies.traceability_summary: Limited ingredient-level deforestation commitments. Focus is on packaging sustainability.

incidents.summary: Ruled that Innocent (Coca-Cola subsidiary) misled consumers about environmental impact of its products

companies.ngos_score: Ranked world's worst plastic polluter multiple years. Ethical Consumer: below benchmark for Innocent.

companies.name: Monde Nissin
companies.traceability_summary: Quorn mycoprotein produced via UK fermentation. Palm oil (in other Quorn products) is RSPO Segregated. Soy is deforestation-free and organic.

companies.name: Nestlé
companies.deforestation_policy: Nestlé Responsible Sourcing Standard + Nescafé Plan
companies.traceability_summary: 96.1% of coffee traceable to first collection point. Satellite monitoring in sourcing regions. Mixed track record across commodities.

incidents.summary: Linked to deforestation in Liberia through cocoa supply chain (alongside Mondelēz, Mars)

companies.ngos_score: Mixed — strong policies on paper, repeated criticism over implementation

companies.name: Ecotone (formerly Wessanen)
companies.traceability_summary: Fairtrade certification provides cooperative-level traceability. Organic adds environmental requirements.
companies.ngos_score: Strong reputation — B Corp certified, focused exclusively on organic and natural food

companies.name: Arla Foods
companies.deforestation_policy: Arla Climate Check and Sustainability Commitments
companies.traceability_summary: Milk traceable to member farms. Feed-chain soy traceability is less transparent.
companies.ngos_score: European dairy industry broadly criticised for soy feed dependency

companies.name: Warburtons Ltd
companies.traceability_summary: RSPO Mass Balance for palm oil. Limited public disclosure on soy sourcing. Family-owned, less sustainability reporting than public companies.

companies.name: Mars Inc.
companies.deforestation_policy: Mars Cocoa for Generations + Responsible Palm Oil Policy
companies.traceability_summary: 100% cocoa traceable to first purchase point. Rainforest Alliance certified. Palm oil 99%+ traceable to mill level. Cocoa & Forests Initiative signatory.

incidents.summary: Linked to deforestation in Liberia through cocoa supply chains

companies.ngos_score: Chocolate Scorecard: middling — better than some, below Tony's and Ben & Jerry's

companies.name: Two Farmers
companies.traceability_summary: Potatoes grown, harvested, stored, cooked and packed on their own Herefordshire farm. Uses sunflower oil — no palm oil. 100% compostable packaging.
companies.ngos_score: Good Shopping Guide: top-rated ethical crisps brand

companies.name: Island Bakery Organics
companies.traceability_summary: All ingredients organic and fully traceable. Palm oil free. Factory powered by hydro-electric turbine and wind turbine on the Isle of Mull. Ovens heated by local wood chip.
companies.ngos_score: Good Shopping Guide: top-rated ethical biscuits. Ethical Consumer: best buy.

companies.name: Bird & Wild Coffee
companies.traceability_summary: 100% shade-grown coffee from agroforestry systems. Triple certified: Fairtrade, Organic, and Smithsonian Bird Friendly (the gold standard for biodiversity-friendly coffee). Coffee grows under native tree canopy, preserving forest habitat.
companies.ngos_score: Ethical Consumer: recommended. Only UK brand with Bird Friendly certification.

companies.name: Biona (Windmill Organics)
companies.traceability_summary: Organic ingredients. Uses organic palm oil where used (RSPO certified). Chocolate spread range is palm oil free, using sunflower oil instead.
companies.ngos_score: Ethical Consumer: recommended for palm oil policy

products.name: Nutella
risk_factors: Palm oil is a primary ingredient (~20%)
Sources from Malaysia and Indonesia
mitigating_factors: RSPO Segregated — strongest available palm oil certification
WWF ranked Ferrero #1 for sustainable palm oil
Traceable to mill level with independent monitoring

products.name: Cadbury Dairy Milk
risk_factors: Cocoa is the primary ingredient from high-risk West Africa
2025 Global Witness linked Mondelēz to Liberia deforestation
Cocoa Life is in-house, not independent like Fairtrade
Mass balance purchasing allows mixing of certified and uncertified cocoa
mitigating_factors: 100% Dairy Milk cocoa from Cocoa Life programme
$1bn investment committed through 2030
Farm-level GPS mapping in major sourcing regions

products.name: Tony's Chocolonely Milk Chocolate
risk_factors: Cocoa is inherently a deforestation-risk commodity
mitigating_factors: 99.95% of supply chain verified deforestation-free
100% traceable to cooperatives with individual farmer GPS mapping
Chocolate Scorecard #1 for five consecutive years
Pays Living Income Reference Price — reduces pressure to expand into forest

products.name: Pringles Original
risk_factors: Palm oil in vegetable oil blend
Mixed certification — Mass Balance allows mixing certified/uncertified
Historical reliance on palm oil credits rather than physical certification
Amnesty International linked China operations to problematic plantations
mitigating_factors: Global Deforestation Policy covers palm oil, soy, paper, sugar
Independent auditor (Proforest) engaged
Palm oil is not the dominant ingredient

products.name: McVitie's Digestives Original
risk_factors: Palm oil is the primary fat in the product
Certification level not clearly specified
Parent company rated worst by Ethical Consumer in biscuits
Limited public transparency compared to peers
mitigating_factors: Earthworm Foundation partnership since 2018
Starling satellite monitoring for deforestation detection
Formal No Deforestation policy

products.name: Green & Black's Organic Dark 85%
risk_factors: 85% cocoa — entirely dependent on a deforestation-risk commodity
Parent company Mondelēz linked to deforestation (Global Witness 2025)
2019 Channel 4 found child labour and deforestation in supplying farms
Some G&B product lines have dropped Fairtrade
mitigating_factors: This specific product carries both Fairtrade and Organic certification
Fairtrade provides independent third-party verification
No palm oil in this product
Diverse sourcing regions

products.name: Pot Noodle Chicken & Mushroom
risk_factors: Palm oil used to fry noodles — integral to product
No product-specific certification on packaging
Gap between Unilever's corporate claims and product-level verification
Contains soy
mitigating_factors: Unilever has one of the most comprehensive deforestation policies in FMCG
97% of key commodities independently verified deforestation-free (corporate level)
Founding RSPO member with satellite monitoring

products.name: Ben & Jerry's Cookie Dough
risk_factors: Cocoa is a deforestation-risk commodity
Parent company Unilever criticised over palm oil suppliers
mitigating_factors: Cocoa via Tony's Open Chain — industry-leading traceability
Multiple Fairtrade ingredients
B Corp certified
No palm oil in this product

products.name: Innocent Strawberries & Bananas Smoothie
risk_factors: Parent company Coca-Cola has poor environmental record
ASA ruled environmental marketing claims were misleading
mitigating_factors: No high-risk deforestation commodities in this product
B Corp certified
Product is just crushed fruit

products.name: Quorn Mince
risk_factors: Parent company has a broader portfolio with less scrutiny
mitigating_factors: Zero deforestation-risk commodities
Mycoprotein produced via UK fermentation — minimal land use
Carbon Trust certified footprint

products.name: Nescafé Original Instant Coffee
risk_factors: Coffee is 100% of the product
Instant coffee typically uses sun-grown beans
Sourced from high-deforestation-risk countries
Rainforest Alliance not consistently applied to this product
mitigating_factors: 96.1% of coffee volume traceable
Nescafé Plan invests in agroforestry
Satellite monitoring in sourcing regions

products.name: Clipper Everyday Tea
risk_factors: Tea has some deforestation linkage, though weaker than other commodities
mitigating_factors: Dual Fairtrade and Organic certification
Tea is inherently lower-risk than palm oil or cocoa
Parent company Ecotone is B Corp certified

products.name: Lurpak Slightly Salted Butter
risk_factors: Dairy cows fed imported soy from deforestation-risk regions
Indirect link is difficult to trace
Red Tractor doesn't address feed-chain deforestation
mitigating_factors: No direct deforestation-risk commodities in the product itself
Arla has formal sustainability commitments
The indirect link is shared by virtually all conventional dairy

products.name: Warburtons Toastie White Bread
risk_factors: Contains both palm oil and soy
Mass Balance certification (weaker traceability)
Limited public disclosure on soy sourcing
mitigating_factors: Both commodities are minor ingredients
Primary ingredient (wheat) is UK-sourced
RSPO membership

products.name: Galaxy Smooth Milk Chocolate
risk_factors: Cocoa and palm oil both present
2025 Global Witness linked Mars to Liberia deforestation
Rainforest Alliance has been criticised for not fully preventing deforestation
mitigating_factors: 100% Rainforest Alliance certified cocoa
Cocoa & Forests Initiative signatory
Palm oil nearly fully traceable to mill level

products.name: Two Farmers Lightly Salted Crisps
risk_factors: Small-scale production — limited independent scrutiny compared to major brands
mitigating_factors: Zero deforestation-risk commodities — potatoes and sunflower oil only
Entirely UK-grown and produced on their own farm
100% compostable packaging
Powered by renewable energy

products.name: Island Bakery Organic Shortbread
risk_factors: Small brand — less public scrutiny and reporting than major manufacturers
mitigating_factors: Zero deforestation-risk commodities — no palm oil, no soy, no cocoa
Fully organic and traceable ingredients
Factory powered by hydro and wind on the Isle of Mull
Palm oil free
Top-rated by Good Shopping Guide and Ethical Consumer

products.name: Bird & Wild Shade Roast Coffee
risk_factors: Coffee is inherently a deforestation-risk commodity
mitigating_factors: 100% shade-grown under native tree canopy — actively preserves forest
Triple certified: Fairtrade, Organic, and Bird Friendly
Bird Friendly is the gold standard for biodiversity in coffee — requires 100% organic shade-grown
Agroforestry model means coffee production protects rather than destroys forest

products.name: Biona Organic Dark Chocolate Spread
risk_factors: Contains cocoa — a deforestation-risk commodity
Small brand with less public supply chain disclosure than major manufacturers
mitigating_factors: Palm oil free — uses sunflower oil instead
Organic certified

Cocoa amount is moderate, not dominant
